summary: Good‑quality C Miller soundboard capture of a solid September‑87 show, highlighted by an extended Black Peter and strong second set despite some first‑set mix hiccups.
review: The Miller remaster presents a clear soundboard recording that is generally clean, though reviewers note occasional muddiness in the opening songs and a guitar‑mix issue during Tennessee Jed. Musically the concert shines with a soulful, extended Black Peter and a second set that “rips,” while the first set suffers from a few technical glitches and a shaky rendition of Queen Jane.

ai_rating.rationale: Average reviewer rating ≈3.6, but most comments are positive about the performance and recording; weighting favors show quality, yielding a solid above‑average score.

recording_quality.technical_notes: C Miller remaster is crisp overall; first set has some mix problems (muddy ambience, guitar dropping out on Tennessee Jed) but the second half sounds clear and balanced.

show_quality.setlist_flow: Strong opening with Brent digging into ‘Hey Pockey Way’, but the first set is marred by a few technical slips. The second set picks up momentum and delivers an energetic, ripping performance.

band_member_comments.Jerry: His guitar drops out on Tennessee Jed and sounds buried in the mix early, but overall tone is solid.
band_member_comments.Keys: Brent (keyboards) is noted for digging into ‘Hey Pockey Way’ and adding energy.
